text.title: Go Project Structure Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying go-project-structure. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix keyed to the methodology.

antipattern.symptom: cmd/api/main.go grows to 500 lines including HTTP handlers and DB queries.
antipattern.root-cause: Author kept adding 'just one more' until main.go became the whole app.
antipattern.fix: Extract handlers + service + repo into internal/. Reduce main.go to wiring (config, deps, app.Run).

antipattern.symptom: pkg/auth/ used only inside this module; external consumers can import it and create accidental dependencies.
antipattern.root-cause: Author copied a tutorial that used pkg/ for everything.
antipattern.fix: Move to internal/auth/. Verify external consumers don't break (they shouldn't have been importing it).

antipattern.symptom: Build fails with 'import cycle not allowed' after internal/orders and internal/payments both grew to import each other.
antipattern.fix: Invert one side via an interface declared at consumer. Resolve cycle, document the boundary.

antipattern.symptom: Project root contains scripts/, tools/, helpers/, scratch/ — many overlapping.
antipattern.root-cause: No top-level discipline; each helper added a new dir.
antipattern.fix: Consolidate to Makefile orchestration. Move runnable helpers to cmd/<helper>/. Delete one-off scripts after capturing intent in Make targets.
